findings.message: PII logged: the owner's email address is written to the application log in the item_created event. The Item model's own comment explicitly states owner_email is PII and must not be included in logs. Emails in logs leak into log aggregation/retention systems beyond the stated creation purpose.
findings.suggestion: Drop owner_email from the log call. Log only non-PII identifiers for the audit event, e.g. log.info("item_created", item_id=item.id). If owner attribution is required, log a non-reversible reference (e.g. a hashed owner id) rather than the raw email.

findings.message: PII collected without being used for the stated purpose: ItemCreate accepts owner_email, but create_item(session, payload.name) never persists it — the value is only routed into a log line. Collecting an email that is not stored or used for the item-creation purpose is unnecessary PII collection.
findings.suggestion: Either remove owner_email from ItemCreate if it is not actually needed, or persist it through create_item for a documented purpose with appropriate access controls — do not collect it solely to emit it to logs.
